meaning: adj.浣挎竻閱掔殑,浣垮喎闈欑殑
examples: It is sobering to realize that this is not a new problem.
It is a sobering thought that in the 17th century she could have been burned as a witch.
Their sobering conversation brought home to everyone present the serious and worthwhile work the Red Cross does.
Perhaps most sobering for U.S. taxpayers and creditors are some robust assumptions made in the report.
Here's one more sobering oil fact: The world has only a 1% short-term cushion.
